none: Joshua Tree National Monument (N.M.), Proposed wilderness area : environmental impact statement.

The National Park Service proposed designating 372,700 acres within Joshua Tree National Monument in California as a wilderness area, with an additional 66,800 acres as potential wilderness. This proposal aimed to protect plant and wildlife communities and maintain a consistent management policy. However, it would limit motorized access and development, affecting visitor use. Alternatives included no designation, increased wilderness, or decreased wilderness. The proposal was part of a broader effort to preserve the area's natural beauty and provide recreational opportunities while balancing environmental protection.
Joshua Tree National Monument (N.M.), Proposed wilderness area : environmental impact statement.

The National Park Service proposed designating 372,700 acres within Joshua Tree National Monument in California as a wilderness area, with an additional 66,800 acres as potential wilderness. This proposal aimed to protect plant and wildlife communities and maintain a consistent management policy. However, it would limit motorized access and development, affecting visitor use. Alternatives included no designation, increased wilderness, or decreased wilderness. The proposal was part of a broader effort to preserve the area's natural beauty and provide recreational opportunities while balancing environmental protection.

The proposal to designate a large portion of Joshua Tree National Monument as a wilderness area was driven by the need to protect its unique ecosystems and biodiversity. While the initiative aimed to enhance conservation efforts, it faced some public pushback from those concerned about restricted access and potential impacts on recreational activities. Ultimately, the proposal was part of a larger movement to ensure the preservation of natural landscapes in the face of increasing development pressures.
